frameworks.name: cortical excitability
frameworks.one_line: V1-V3 resting activity and excitability gate sensory imagery strength.

frameworks.name: distributed imagery network
frameworks.one_line: Prefrontal, parietal, temporal, and visual areas support image generation.

core_question: How does the imagery network control the strength of visual mental imagery?
key_assumptions: Mental imagery recruits early visual cortex
Phosphene threshold indexes visual cortex excitability
tDCS can causally modulate visual cortex excitability
manipulations: Measured resting activity and excitability in early visual cortex (V1-V3)
Applied tDCS to decrease visual cortex excitability
key_findings: Lower resting activity in early visual cortex predicts stronger imagery
Lower cortical excitability (phosphene threshold) predicts stronger imagery
tDCS-induced decrease in visual cortex excitability increases imagery strength
interpretations: Visual cortex excitability causally controls visual imagery strength
A neurophysiological mechanism of cortical excitability underlies imagery strength
